hypothesis: If, in semi-supervised learning on molecular graphs, each model in an ensemble minimizes its supervised loss plus a gradient-detached consistency loss to the ensemble mean on unlabeled data, then test accuracy/regression error and calibration will surpass supervised, mean-teacher, and PSEUDOσ baselines and improve with larger ensemble size across QM9 and GNN+ benchmarks, because consensus targets reduce variance and confirmation bias in pseudo-labels.
